error_signature: JSON parameter needs to be valid JSON [item 0]
title: Invalid JSON format in HTTP request
symptom: When running a workflow with an HTTP request node, the error 'JSON parameter needs to be valid JSON [item 0]' appears. This indicates that the JSON being sent is not properly formatted.
not_this_if: Error occurs in a different node
root_cause: The error occurs because the JSON being constructed includes extra quotation marks around expressions, causing it to be invalid. This typically happens when using templating expressions without proper formatting.
fix_steps: Identify the JSON parameters in the HTTP request node that are causing the error.
Remove any extra quotation marks around expressions and ensure they are wrapped in JSON.stringify() where necessary.
Test the workflow again to confirm the error is resolved.
agent_instruction: Ensure all expressions in the JSON are properly formatted and wrapped in JSON.stringify() as needed.
